component: validation scripts and Playwright webServer output
impact: The normal validation sequence completed with repeated third-party ExperimentalWarning snippets, making local and CI logs look noisy even when checks passed.
rootCause: Newer Node/npm toolchains emitted ExperimentalWarning messages when npm's bundled debug package required supports-color as ESM, and ESLint legacy eslintrc loading emitted the same warning when @eslint/eslintrc required svelte-eslint-parser. Nested npm/npx invocations and Playwright webServer startup repeated the warning across lint, a11y lint, ESLint, and grouped E2E preview output.
resolution: Added a narrow Node warning preload that drops only the known npm/debug/supports-color and @eslint/eslintrc/svelte-eslint-parser ExperimentalWarning messages, routed lint/build/Playwright child processes through that preload, and replaced recurring nested npm/npx validation invocations with direct node-based tool calls where practical. Existing ESLintRCWarning suppression remains in place, and real lint, type, build, test, console error, and Playwright failures continue to surface normally.
